home.headline: The Digital Library for the Next Generation of Teachers
home.description: Textbooks, research papers, and teaching resources — carefully curated, open and free for every student and educator.
home.ctaBody: 100+ educational resources — textbooks, research papers, and teaching guides — available free to every educator in Cambodia.
home.heroSubtitle: Access thousands of books, research papers, and educational resources.
home.discoverLatest: Discover the latest research insights, library announcements, and educational resources from our community.
home.searchPlaceholder: Search title, author, ISBN, or topic
home.prompt1: Find a book about teaching methods…
home.askHint: Search e-books, physical books, and theses — or press / to start typing.
home.constellationHint: Focus the search field to see the collection light up.
home.bentoEbooksBody: Read anywhere — every e-book is free for PTEC students and teachers, on any device.
home.aboutLibraryBody: PTEC was established in 2017 by merging two teacher-training centers, with support from JICA. The library is home to the Department of Educational Research and Library — built for Cambodia's next generation of teachers.
home.researchBody: Read PTEC's research bulletin and reports by our own lecturers and student teachers.
home.aboutLede: The Heart of Learning and Research for Excellence in 21st Century Teacher Education
home.aboutSupport: The Department of Educational Research and Library at PTEC provides quality information resources, library services, and learning spaces — supporting Cambodia’s educators in teaching, research, and innovation since 2017.
home.aboutAccessValue: Open access — no account, no paywall
home.aboutFeatCuratedBody: Over 30 educator-authored titles and 4 research bulletins published since 2017.
home.aboutFeatOpenBody: Collection spans Khmer, English, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, and Thai — serving every reader.
home.aboutFeatBilingualBody: Theses, action research reports, graduation reports, and educational journals for researchers.
home.aboutFeatCampusBody: Resources organized by Dewey Decimal Classification for systematic and intuitive navigation.
home.authorsWorksLabel: Works by {name}, {count, plural, one {# work} other {# works}}
home.howStep1Title: Search or browse — no account needed
home.howStep1Body: Search books, theses, and publications in English or Khmer, or browse by subject. Everything is free and open.
home.howStep2Title: Read online or save for offline
home.howStep2Body: Read any PDF right in your browser, or save it to your device and keep reading without internet.
home.howStep3Body: A free account lets you save books, track reading progress, write reviews, and get alerts when new resources arrive.
home.faqQ1: Is the library free to use?
home.faqA1: Yes. The PTEC Digital Library is a free public library — reading and downloading digital resources costs nothing, and no payment is ever required.
home.faqQ2: Can I download books to read offline?
home.faqA2: Yes. Books with a PDF can be saved to your device and read without an internet connection. Your saved books are listed on the Offline books page.
home.faqA3: No account is needed to search, browse, or read. A free account adds extras: saving books, tracking your reading progress, writing reviews, and new-arrival alerts.
home.faqQ4: How do I borrow a physical book?
home.faqA4: Check Books in Library to see each title's availability and shelf location, then visit the library desk during opening hours to borrow it.
home.faqA5: Yes. The collection includes resources in both Khmer and English, and the whole website can be used in either language.
home.faqQ6: How do I submit my thesis or research?
home.faqA6: Theses are added to the repository by library staff. Contact the library team and they will guide you through submitting your work.
home.goalsTitle: What do you want to do today?
home.goalsBody: Pick a starting point — each one leads to real resources already in the library.
home.goalLessonBody: Lesson plans, teaching methods and classroom materials.
home.goalThesisBody: Past theses, research reports and writing guides.
home.goalResearchBody: Research methods, academic writing and data literacy.
home.goalTeacherBody: Guided learning paths for professional growth.
home.goalKhmerBody: Books and materials in the Khmer language.
home.weekEmpty: New highlights are being curated — check back soon.
home.popularOnboarding: Sign in free to track your reading and pick up where you left off.
home.libraryNowTitle: Read online, or visit us on campus
home.libraryNowBody: Every resource is free online, anytime. The physical library is open during these hours.
home.libraryNowDigitalBody: Read and download every resource online — free, on any device.
home.libraryNowHoursNote: Regular hours — public holidays may differ.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: The PTEC e-Library is a free digital library operated by the Phnom Penh Teacher Education College. We collect only the personal data needed to run the library, we never sell it, and you can delete your account and data at any time. This page explains exactly what we store and why.
privacy.collectAccount: Account information: your email address, display name, and an optional profile photo. If you sign in with Google, we receive your name, email, and Google profile photo.
privacy.collectActivity: Library activity: books you save, reading lists, reading progress, personal notes and highlights, and reviews you write. This is what makes features like “continue reading” work.
privacy.collectContact: Contact form messages: the name, email, and message you send us are forwarded to library staff so we can reply, and are not used for anything else.
privacy.collectTechnical: Technical records: download logs and search queries, used to keep the service secure and to improve the catalog (for example, finding searches that return no results).
privacy.notBody: We do not sell or share your personal data with advertisers, we do not show ads, and we do not track you across other websites. We do not collect any personal data beyond what is listed above.
privacy.storageBody: Account and activity data are stored in our database (Supabase/PostgreSQL); book files are stored on the library’s file storage. All connections use HTTPS encryption, and access to private data is restricted by server-side permission checks.
privacy.visibilityPrivate: Private to you: personal notes, highlights, reading progress, saved books, and reading lists are visible only to your account. Library staff cannot browse them.
privacy.visibilityPublic: Public: book reviews you post are shown on the book page together with your display name and profile photo.
privacy.retentionBody: Your data is kept while your account exists. When you delete your account, your profile, notes, reading progress, saved lists, and reviews are permanently removed. Contact-form messages are kept only as long as needed to respond.
privacy.choicesBody: You can edit your name and photo, change your password, or permanently delete your account and all associated data from
privacy.contactBody: If you have any question about your data, or want a copy of it, please reach us through the

header.subtitle: Here is what changed across the library.

kpi.uniqueVisitorsDef: Distinct people who viewed a content detail page. Signed-in users are counted once across the period; anonymous visitors are counted per day using a privacy-preserving daily-rotating identifier (no IP addresses are stored).
kpi.detailViewsDef: Detail-page views of books, theses, publications and posts during the selected period. Bot traffic is excluded.
kpi.readerOpensDef: Times a visitor opened the online PDF reader. This event is newly instrumented — history begins when collection started.
kpi.downloadsDef: Completed download requests for book and thesis files during the selected period.
kpi.conversionDef: Identified visitors who opened the reader or downloaded a file, divided by identified visitors who viewed a content detail page, in the selected period.
kpi.periodNote: All five cards show activity in {range} only — lifetime inventory lives in Content → Collection health.
kpi.internalExcluded: Internal staff activity is excluded from these metrics.

engagement.drillTotal: {count, plural, =1 {1 detail view} other {# detail views}} recorded (staff excluded)
engagement.drillEmpty: No detail views were recorded in this period.
engagement.drillError: Could not load the breakdown — please try again.

items.contentDrafts: {count, plural, =1 {1 draft awaits publication} other {# drafts await publication}}

items.contactInbox: {count, plural, =1 {1 unread contact message} other {# unread contact messages}}
items.needsReview: {count, plural, =1 {1 item is waiting in the review queue} other {# items are waiting in the review queue}}
items.zeroResultQueries: {count, plural, =1 {1 search term repeatedly finds nothing} other {# search terms repeatedly find nothing}}
items.lowStock: {count, plural, =1 {1 catalogue title is low on copies} other {# catalogue titles are low on copies}}
items.catalogEmpty: Physical catalogue has no records yet — add your first copies

actionCenter.passed: {count, plural, =1 {1 check passed} other {# checks passed}}

insights.title: Insights
insights.disclaimer: Generated by fixed rules over the metrics above — no AI involvement, and correlations are not causes.
insights.viewsUpConversionDown: Views grew {viewsGrowthPct}% but reader-open conversion fell from {convPrevPct}% to {convNowPct}% — more people are finding content without reading it.
insights.zeroResultRepeats: {terms} search terms repeatedly returned no results this period.
insights.newContentNoViews: {count} resources published this period have not been viewed yet.
insights.deptDemandLowCoverage: “{dept}” gets {viewsPerResource} views per resource from only {resources, plural, =1 {# resource} other {# resources}} — demand outstrips the collection.
insights.khmerSearchGrowth: Khmer searches grew to {nowPct}% of all searches (from {prevPct}%).

content.emptyPreset: No content matches this view for the selected period and filters.
content.deptFormula: Normalised as activity ÷ published resources in that department, so small departments compare fairly against large ones.
content.deptEmpty: No department activity in this period.
content.metaFormula: Metadata completeness — the share of key catalogue fields filled in (cover, description, author, language, department, year, …)

health.title: Collection health
health.lifetimeNote: Lifetime totals — unaffected by the date range
health.catalogEmptyHint: No physical copies are tracked yet. Add catalogue records to manage shelf stock.

searchAi.kmShareHint: Share of searches typed in Khmer
searchAi.noNoClick: No ignored result sets in this period.
searchAi.aiCollecting: AI request telemetry starts collecting after this deployment — success rate and latency will appear here.
searchAi.opportunitiesHint: Deterministic rules over search demand — repeated zero-result terms and high-demand topics with thin results.
searchAi.noOpportunities: No collection gaps detected from search behaviour in this period.
searchAi.trendSubtitle: Searches per day with the zero-result share
searchAi.trendSr: {total} searches in total, {zero} returned no results.
searchAi.testQueryNote: Rows marked “suspected test” match a deterministic automation heuristic and are excluded from collection opportunities — they remain in raw analytics.
searchAi.aiCollectingShort: Collecting telemetry — success rate and latency appear after the next deployment.
searchAi.dismissFailed: Could not dismiss the term. Please try again.

audience.newRegistrationsDef: Accounts created during the selected period (not the cumulative total).
audience.activeUsersDef: Signed-in users with any view, reader or download activity during the period.
audience.returningUsersDef: Signed-in users active on two or more different days during the period.
audience.identifiedVisitorsDef: Distinct visitors countable this period: signed-in users plus anonymous daily sessions. No IP addresses or durable anonymous identifiers are stored.
audience.localeCollecting: Language tracking is newly instrumented — data appears as new views are recorded.
audience.privacyNote: Anonymous visitors are counted with a daily-rotating hash: one person returning across days counts once per day. Raw IP addresses are never stored, and device-level tracking is not performed.
audience.smallDataNote: Small dataset ({count} public detail views in this period) — read shares as indicative, not conclusive.

system.storageCollecting: Storage telemetry starts collecting after this deployment — Zima success/failure and R2 fallback usage will appear here.
system.aiCollecting: No AI requests recorded in this period yet.
system.backupNotTracked: Not tracked by this application yet
system.telemetryCollectingNote: Storage and AI telemetry begin collecting after the next deployment — statuses show “Collecting” until then.
system.backupSetupHint: Backup logging is not set up — see docs/BACKUP-DR.md

states.sectionErrorHint: The rest of the dashboard is unaffected. Retry to reload the data.

overview.collectionLinkHint: Inventory totals, metadata gaps and the full performance table live in the Content view.

discovery.title: Discovery & engagement
discovery.subtitle: Independent activity streams · {range}
discovery.conversionDef: Identified visitors who opened the reader or downloaded a file ÷ identified visitors who viewed a content detail page.
discovery.definition: These are independent event volumes — visitors also arrive through direct links, so the streams do not form a sequential funnel. Rates compare only pairs measured on comparable populations.
